title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — intermittent 403s affecting multiple team members definitely sounds like something we need to dig into.

To make sure I understand correctly: when the dashboard loads successfully, everything works as expected with full access to all features? Or were there any permission-related quirks even before the 403s started?

Also, could you walk me through exactly what you're doing when the 403 appears? For example: Are you navigating to the dashboard from a bookmark, clicking a link within TaskFlow, or refreshing an already-open page? Does the 403 happen on the initial page load, or after you interact with something on the dashboard?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: When it does load, everything works fine — I can see all my dashboards and reports like normal. No permission weirdness other than the 403s. It happens no matter how I get there — bookmark, clicking from another page, refreshing. It's pretty random. I'll hit the dashboard, get a 403, refresh the page, and it works. Sometimes it takes a couple refreshes. I honestly thought it was some kind of network issue at first but it's been going on for two days now so figured I should report it.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for the additional detail — the fact that a simple refresh sometimes clears it is a really useful clue.

Could you share some environment details? Specifically:
- What browser (and version) are you using?
- What OS are you on?
- Do you know which version of TaskFlow your team is running (or whether there was a recent deployment around when this started)?

If your teammates who are also seeing this are using different browsers or OSes, that would be helpful to know too.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I'm using Chrome, pretty sure it's the latest version — maybe 124 or something? I'm on Windows 10. As for TaskFlow version, I think we're on 2.3.1 but I'm not 100% sure on that. I do know there was some kind of deployment a couple days ago — right around when this started actually. I'm not sure exactly what changed though, you'd have to check with the ops team on that. As for my teammates, I think at least one of them uses Firefox and they're seeing it too, so I don't think it's a browser thing.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Intermittent 403 Forbidden errors on dashboard after recent deployment","problem":"Multiple team members are randomly receiving 403 Forbidden errors when accessing the TaskFlow dashboard. The error occurs regardless of navigation method (bookmark, in-app link, refresh) and across browsers (Chrome, Firefox). A page refresh sometimes clears the error. The issue started approximately two days ago, coinciding with a deployment.","root_cause_hypothesis":"A recent deployment likely introduced a race condition or misconfiguration in the authorization/session layer. The intermittent nature and resolution on refresh suggest either: (1) a load-balancer routing to inconsistently configured backend instances (e.g., one node has stale auth config), (2) a caching issue where an authorization middleware intermittently serves a cached 403 response, or (3) a session/token validation race condition introduced in the deployment.","reproduction_steps":["Log in to TaskFlow as a user with dashboard access","Navigate to the dashboard (via bookmark, in-app link, or direct URL)","If 403 appears, refresh the page — it may load on retry","Repeat navigation several times; 403 should appear intermittently"],"environment":"Chrome ~124 on Windows 10; also reproduced on Firefox. TaskFlow version believed to be 2.3.1. Issue affects multiple team members across browsers.","severity":"high","impact":"Multiple team members are intermittently blocked from accessing the dashboard, a core feature. While a refresh sometimes works around it, the issue is disruptive and erodes trust in the application's reliability.","recommended_fix":"1. Identify exactly what was deployed ~2 days ago and review changes to auth middleware, session handling, or reverse proxy configuration. 2. Check server-side logs for the 403 responses — determine which component is returning them (application, API gateway, load balancer). 3. If using multiple backend instances, verify consistent deployment and configuration across all nodes. 4. Check for caching headers on auth-related responses that might cause stale 403s to be served.","proposed_test_case":"Automated test that makes 50+ sequential authenticated requests to the dashboard endpoint and asserts that all return 200 (not 403) for a user with valid permissions. Run this against each backend instance individually to identify inconsistent nodes.","information_gaps":["Exact deployment changes made ~2 days ago (reporter doesn't know; ops team would)","Server-side logs showing the source of the 403 responses","Whether the 403 is returned by the application, a reverse proxy, or an API gateway","Exact TaskFlow version (reporter said ~2.3.1 but was unsure)"]}
